transcript: In a small town, numerous men pass away after not resisting the temptation of making love with beautiful women. After the death of Brandt's bacteriologist Jack Grubowski, State Department Special Agent Neil Agar visits the Sheriff's Office and introduces himself to Captain Peters. Immediately, he requests Grubowski's file, wanting to start his investigation. As Peters finds the file, he converses with Neil about Grubowski's case, and the agent opines that it's easy. Peter agrees with his opinion, but he wonders why the government wants to meddle with such a case. For Neil, since Grubowski was a bacteriologist in Brandt, a government-sponsored research facility, the government has to keep watch of its affairs. Due to his argument, Peters reveals that another body was found, but the casualty was not affiliated with Brandt. Instead, the body belongs to a factory worker who passed away due to extreme exhaustion, like Grubowski. With his revelation, Neil becomes more intrigued by the ongoing case. A while later, Neil heads to the Brandt Research Facility to search for clues regarding the case. As he scans the library, he bumps into the librarian named Julie Zorn, causing her to drop some of the books she's carrying. Neil quickly helps her gather the books and apologizes for the trouble he's caused. While at it, he asks Julie who's in charge of the library, and she answers that she is. Neil gladly introduces himself and informs her about the purpose of his sudden visit, to investigate Grubowski's death. At first, Julie is apprehensive as the file he needs is highly confidential, but when Neil threatens her with his rank, she eventually yields. Inside the librarian's office, Neil rummages through her table while she fetches the file for him. Neil sees Grubowski's photo in one of her drawers and studies it intently. But Julie returns. She snatches the photo from Neil's hands and reprimands him for snooping through her things. To justify his actions, Neil reasons it's part of his job, and she's a person of interest since she was with Grubowski on the night of his passing. This leads Julie to confirm they did have dinner as it's part of their routine. Interestingly, Grubowski's wife, Gretchen, knows about their frequent dinners, piquing Neil's interest. Then, Julie further reveals that they went to dinner at the Flamingo Bar and Grill. After that, they made love in a motel. With her revelation, Neil wants to know more, so he invites Julie to have lunch with him. Meanwhile, a man takes a seductive woman on a grassy field to make love to her. As the man enjoys her company, he suddenly becomes unresponsive. It turns out the woman has drained away his life. During their lunch, Julie shares that most of Grubowski's friends are womanizers and they have an active, intimate life. Suddenly, a scientist stands up, catching Neil's attention. Julie also notices him, so she informs Neil about the man's identity. She shares that he is Dr. Merger, the head of reproductive biology. However, she finds it ironic that a man specializing in reproduction is a loner, and he's never seen with a woman. This information sparks Neil's curiosity, so he asks Julie if he's straight, but she doesn't know. Later that evening, Neil joins Grubowski's friends in a bar and gets acquainted with them. To start their conversation, Neil identifies Dr. Herb Klein as the head of strategic alternatives, making the scientist happy that he knows him. Then, the next scientist introduces himself as Dr. Stan Williams, the head of genetics. Afterward, he takes the honor of introducing the last scientist in the circle, Dr. Aldo Ferrara, head of the Department of Diagnostics and Clinical Biometrics. Once the introductions are exchanged, Stan expresses his regret for Grabowski's passing, wishing that he is the one in his position. Before Neil can speak, an alluring woman walks Thank you. tricks. Once the introductions are exchanged, Stan expresses his regret for Grabowski's passing, wishing that he is the one in his position. Before Neil can speak, an alluring woman walks into the bar and sits near their table. Neil asks the men who the woman is, and Stan tells him she is Dr. Susan Harris, Brandt's ice queen. This time, Aldo informs him of her specialty, and he reveals that she is an entomologist. Upon learning that, Neil gives her a flirty gesture, earning a chuckle from the men as they know it'll be fruitless. Meanwhile, a morning Gretchen receives a phone call from an unknown caller who informs her that she was with Grubowski in a hotel moments before his passing. Gretchen tries to argue, but the caller urges her to meet with her as she has important things to say regarding her husband. Left with no choice, Gretchen agrees to meet. One day in the coroner's office, Neil and Captain Peters encounter another body that has suffered congestive heart failure due to exhaustion from making love. Neil, Peters, and the coroner are baffled as to how the casualties have met their demise when all of them have no history of heart disease. Due to this, Neil has no choice but to call the public health service. The next day, Captain Peters holds a public meeting regarding the men's rising mortality rate in their town. He discloses that all of them passed away after getting exhausted from making love. Upon hearing this, the locals laugh, making Captain Peters uneasy. Moving on to his speech, Peter shares that Dr. Merger opines that a virulent form of venereal disease caused their passing. For him to share this idea, Peter gives the floor to him. Dr. Merger expresses his wonder about the unusual passing that might be linked to a venereal disease. Therefore, he proposes that a curfew must be implemented. Moreover, he urges everyone to practice abstinence to make his intervention even more effective. One of the men in the audience named Barney Braddock angrily objects to his proposal, arguing that it's a ridiculous idea especially since they're still unsure of the real cause of the casualty's passing. For him, it's probably Brandt's fault as they might have failed an experiment. Furthermore, he can't simply give up the pleasure of being intimate with his wife for them. With his ego brushed due to the locals' approval, Barney threatens to leave the meeting, but Captain Peters tries to convince him to stay. However, he remains firm and continues to insult them. Later that evening, a drunk Barney leaves the bar when a seductive woman stops him in his tracks. It turns out to be Gretchen, dressed differently than she's used to. She asks Barney if she can accompany him for the night, making him ecstatically approach her unaware of the danger he'll get himself into. Meanwhile, Neil and Julie drive to where Dr. Merger told them he'd meet them. While they wait for him, Neil tries to kiss Julie, but the woman immediately shares her theory about the case. However, Neil finds it ridiculous, so he asks her if she believes in such a theory. But Julie laughs and denies it. After sharing a laugh, their mood switches and Neil can't hold back anymore. Therefore, he kisses her and Julie reciprocates. While the two are having their moment, Dr. Merger is running for his life as a car tails him. He stumbles in panic, knocking over the trash bin. This alerts the couple, making them end their moment and investigate the noise. Dr. Merger tries to climb up a broken car but slips, allowing the driver to ram him. Though severely injured, he tries to escape, but the driver ruthlessly runs him over, ending his life. Upon witnessing such tragedy, Neil orders Julie to call Captain Peters while he tries to chase the culprit. Shortly after, Peters and his men are at the scene of the crime, investigating. Just in time, Neil arrives and Julie rushes over to him, checking if he's alright. After confirming he is, Peters calls his attention, asking if he's found the driver. Neil says no, but he's found the car, empty. Therefore, Neil declares that the total number of male casualties in the town is nine, but Peters tells him it's ten. Neil's intrigued by the sudden addition, so he approaches Peters. Degrudgingly, Peters reveals that Barney's passed away, evidently after making love. Now that the mood's spoiled, Neil moves on and suggests that Peters send his men to identify the owner of the car. However, Peters takes this wrong and expresses his annoyance at government agents like him as he feels he's treated inferiorly. Neil chuckles at his outburst but clarifies that he is only doing his job and they should focus on accomplishing their tasks. Afterward, he sincerely apologizes to Peters for making him feel insulted. Because of this, Peters' temper subsides, and he apologizes to him. Once their conversation's over, Neil and Julie take their leave. Due to the recent loss of lives, the government issues a lockdown in the town of Peckham. Later that night, Neil investigates Dr. Merger's office when he discovers a button on one of the scientist's shelves. Once he presses it, a door opens, leading to a sensual-looking room. As he scales the area, he notices a pile of open letters addressed to Merger. After reading one of the letters, Neil realizes that Merger has a lover named Joe. Suddenly, Joe emerges from behind and attacks Neil. Despite Joe's strength, Neil's able to defend himself and knock the man unconscious. Shortly afterward, Neil interrogates Joe regarding his whereabouts during Merger's passing. Joe tells him he's been at a bar since Merger postponed their supposed rendezvous, making him hurt and angry. Unsatisfied with his answers, Neil asks him if he has no idea where the scientist was. But Joe supplies that he's seen him with a woman instead of a man. According to him, he saw Merger with a woman in his car earlier, driving through town. Neil asks him what she looks like, but Joe sadly fails to give him the details since it was too dark. But to make it up, he discloses that the woman was driving his lover's car. Due to his revelation, Neil asks him if Merger also likes women, but Joe strongly rejects the idea. The next day, Dr. Susan joins Dr. Herb for breakfast, but she notices that the cafeteria is empty. Herb tells her everyone's locked in their rooms reading novels to pass their time during the lockdown. While they make small talk, Herb observes that Susan continuously adds an unusual amount of sugar to her coffee. In her defense, she's been craving sweets lately, but Herb doesn't like what she's doing. He argues that too much sweets will negatively affect her appetite. Smoothly, he shifts his comment to asking her to have dinner tonight. Immediately, Susan accepts as she finds the recent lockdown boring. While at it, Susan suggests they can be intimate as long as they're discreet. Herb gladly agrees since he likes her idea. Moments later, Herb's preparing for his date when Neil interviews him about his knowledge regarding Grubowski and Merger's lives. Suddenly, he receives a phone call from his wife, so he quickly answers it. His wife asks him to come home early, but he lies that he can't as he's swamped with paperwork. Upon witnessing Herb's blatant cheating, Neil chuckles and simply eavesdrops. After the call, Herb is eager to leave work, so he inquires Neil about his plans for the night. Neil reveals that he'll meet Julie later, causing Herb to commend his choice of woman. Afterward, he asks Neil to wish him good luck as he'll have a date with the Ice Queen. During their dinner, Susan seductively eats her dessert, entrancing Herb. To mask the awkwardness of getting caught, Herb asks if he can smoke, but Susan refuses as she is allergic to it. Instead, Susan offers him her dessert, but Herb declines as he's watching out for his health. However, Susan continuously pleads to try it, so he eventually relents. Gladly, she feeds him with the dessert, and Herb finds himself enjoying it. A few moments later, Susan begins to seduce Herb, and the man unthinkingly falls for it. Unbeknownst to him, he'll be experiencing the same fate as the other casualties of Peckham. On the same night, Neil and Julie watch a documentary about insects' life and reproductive behavior after Captain Peters remarks the men are dropping like flies. They're intrigued to learn that female insects such as the Black Widow and Praying Mantis devour their mates after copulating. On the other hand, Herb's wife Nora receives a call from Susan, threatening that something terrible will happen to her husband if she doesn't comply with her wishes. Helpless, Nora gives in. Back in Julie's room, the two finish watching the documentary, but Neil's still unsatisfied with the information they've gathered. Therefore, he plans to visit Dr. Norstad, the department head for etymological studies. However, her.............................. the documentary, but Neil's still unsatisfied with the information they've gathered. Therefore, he plans to visit Dr. Norstad, the department head for etymological studies. However, Julie informs him that he's away and Susan is his substitute for the time being. In the early morning, Neil visits Susan's office to find more information about the insect's mating behavior, but sadly, Susan refuses to entertain him since she's busy, so she tells him to consult her during regular office hours. Regardless of her orders, Neil refuses to comply as he reasons that he has an urgent matter to discuss with her. Due to his persistence, Susan eventually relents. As he waits for the scientist, Neil scales the office and notices that the room is themed with honeybees. Just in time, Susan emerges from the laboratory, and Neil tells her he must ask a few questions. Once again, Susan declines and tells Neil they're in the middle of an important experiment. Upon hearing we, Neil's confused especially since Dr. Norstad is away. Susan reasons that even though he's not around, she's working from his notes. With a sigh, Susan finally grants Neil's request, but only for a short while. Immediately, Neil inquires about the queen bee's mating frequency. Susan answers that queen bees mate only once, just to reproduce, and after the deed, the queen never mates again. When Neil leaves after getting the information he needs, Susan heads to the radiation room to resume her experiment. It turns out she's working on turning Nora into a bee girl by injecting her with bee DNA, which sedates her. Then, with the help of other b-girls, they cocoon her and place her inside a chamber, where a swarm of bees envelops her. Afterward, the b-girls open her cocoon, and Susan powers the machine that exposes her to radiation. Once she awakens, she possesses black compound eyes, similar to bees. The next day, Neil, alongside Captain Peters, finds Herb's body inside a factory. Peter volunteers to inform his wife about his passing and invites Neil to accompany him. However, Neil declines as he plans to visit Brandt. Shortly afterward, Peters arrives at Herb's residence, and Nora enthusiastically greets him. She quickly invites him inside and offers Peters a drink. Opposite to her enthusiasm, Peters grimly informs her that he'll deliver the sad news. But before he can share it, Nora stalls him, thinking that sharing such news in the early morning is quite shameful. Still, she invites him to sit with her, but Peters tries to decline, as he wants to get this over. Despite his refusal, Nora's persistence gets to him, so he eventually complies. Upon sitting, he quickly reveals that her husband has passed away. Instead of mourning, Nora opines that Herb's passing is unsurprising, especially since he's not cautious. While she explains, Nora becomes touchy towards Peters, making him uncomfortable. Because of this, Peters tries to leave immediately, but Nora stops him, telling him to accompany her for a while. Even with her seduction, Peters remains faithful in doing his job, so he quickly gets up and leaves. Meanwhile in Brandt, Stan angrily storms inside the conference room when he sees Neil with Aldo and Julie. He confronts Neil for having his troop barricade Peckham's roads. In Neil's defense, it's beyond his control, so he can do nothing about it. Despite his reason, Stan is keen on making his troop withdraw, but Julie interrupts him by informing him of Herb's fate. This surprises him, but since the new casualty is another colleague and friend of his, Stan furiously questions Neil why they should stay in town, although calms him down and requests him to listen to what Neil has to say. Once calm, Neil begins to ask him if he can cross a man with a horse. Confused, Stan evades his question as he finds it ridiculous. Since Stan keeps on antagonizing him, Neil shackles Ryze and expresses that he's serious. After realizing that he's let his temper get the best of him, Neil gets straight to the point by inquiring Stan about controlled mutations. However, Stan denies its possibility as he explains that they're not yet scientifically capable of altering genes and chromosomes. Neil acknowledges his statement, but he asks him if they can accelerate the process artificially. Again, Stan believes it's impossible, but Aldo thinks otherwise. Aldo explains his theory of artificially substituting hormones, but Stan negates it. Still, he mentions that maybe Dr. Norstad and his bees might've made it possible. After hearing this, Neil becomes alert and requests the two scientists to help him prove his theory. Moments later, Neil presents his theory, but Stan remains skeptical about it and even deems it a fiction. Neil tries to reason with him, but Stan is too stubborn to listen. When Stan leaves, Aldo comments that Neil's theory has few credible points, returning the man's hope. Because of this, Neil asks if he'll help him, and Aldo confirms that he will. Now finding his determination, Neil orders Aldo to turn his theory into analysis so that he can send it to his agency. Aldo promises to finish it as soon as possible. Still, before letting Neil leave, he opines that if his theory about mutation did occur, it should be detected by a GammaCat synthesizer. Neil chuckles as he has difficulty deciphering what he just said, so Julie explains it. From what she knows, a gamma-count synthesizer works like a camera, and the mutated cells have gamma radiation which registers on a special photosensitive plate. Hence, she strongly suggests they try it to prove Neil's theory. While Neil is still in a meeting with the scientists, they are unaware of another b-girl attack. This time, it happens near the border when a woman asks a soldier for help. Immediately, the soldier follows her, and the woman tells him she has lost her keys. She points toward the possible location of the keys, and the soldier heads to it. Unbeknownst to him, he has fallen prey to a b-girl. During Grubowski's funeral, Neil and Julie notice the deceased men's wives are wearing similar outfits, especially their sunglasses. Julie takes the initiative of using the gamma count synthesizer and successfully detects gamma radiation from them. Inside Stan's residence, his wife Harriet tries to seduce him. Since their marriage lacks intimacy, Stan immediately falls for her charms. While in the act, Stan realizes that Harriet is a b-girl upon seeing her black compound eyes. Before she can end his life, Stan grabs her tights and suffocates her with it. After the funeral, Julie studies the information she gathered earlier when Susan visits her. Julie tries to act nonchalant, but Susan reveals she's aware of what she's discovered, so she wants to explain it to her. Regardless, Julie refuses to listen as she believes her explanation won't suffice. Because of Julie's stubbornness, Susan entices her to visit her lab, promising her she'll be satisfied with what she'll show her. Convinced, Julie follows Susan to her laboratory. On the other hand, Neil's currently at Aldo's residence listening to his voice recorder about the insect's mating habits. Suddenly, Neil hears Susan's voice in the recording, making him realize that the culprit for Aldo's passing was her. Because of this, he also realizes that Julie's life is at risk, and Susan is on a rampage to eliminate those who hinder her plans. As a result, Neil rushes to Brand and arrives just in time to stop Susan from turning Julie into a b-girl. However, Susan threatens to pull the lever that will end Julie's life if he doesn't leave the laboratory. Neil acts like he is leaving, but he immediately pulls out his gun and shoots the machinery, causing chaos inside the room. Neil grabs the unconscious Julie, but before he leaves, he traps the women inside the radiation chamber, ending their lives. Inside the sheriff's office, Peters interrogates Julie and is confused about the motive behind the b-girl's killing spree. Julie explains that Susan overlooked the fact that the radiation caused them to be infertile. But since they share DNA with bees who mate to reproduce, they've become crazy about mating as they can't produce offspring. After Julie's explanation, Neil is in a rush to take her home, so Peters dismisses them immediately. Before he can leave, Peters shakes his hands and expresses his hope to see him again, not as an agent but as a tourist. Shortly after, the couple share a meal inside Julie's residence, but Neil itches to bed her. However, Julie continuously talks about the events earlier, so he quickly gets up and lets her follow him. After arriving at the bedroom, Julie's still talking non-stop, leaving Neil to throw her on the bed and kiss her.

Regardless of her orders, Neil refuses to comply as he reasons that he has an urgent matter to discuss with her. Due to his persistence, Susan eventually relents. As he waits for the scientist, Neil scales the office and notices that the room is themed with honeybees. Just in time, Susan emerges from the laboratory, and Neil tells her he must ask a few questions. Once again, Susan declines and tells Neil they're in the middle of an important experiment. Upon hearing we, Neil's confused especially since Dr. Norstad is away. Susan reasons that even though he's not around, she's working from his notes. With a sigh, Susan finally grants Neil's request, but only for a short while. Immediately, Neil inquires about the queen bee's mating frequency. Susan answers that queen bees mate only once, just to reproduce, and after the deed, the queen never mates again. When Neil leaves after getting the information he needs, Susan heads to the radiation room to resume her experiment. It turns out she's working on turning Nora into a bee girl by injecting her with bee DNA, which sedates her. Then, with the help of other b-girls, they cocoon her and place her inside a chamber, where a swarm of bees envelops her. Afterward, the b-girls open her cocoon, and Susan powers the machine that exposes her to radiation. Once she awakens, she possesses black compound eyes, similar to bees. The next day, Neil, alongside Captain Peters, finds Herb's body inside a factory. Peter volunteers to inform his wife about his passing and invites Neil to accompany him. However, Neil declines as he plans to visit Brandt. Shortly afterward, Peters arrives at Herb's residence, and Nora enthusiastically greets him. She quickly invites him inside and offers Peters a drink. Opposite to her enthusiasm, Peters grimly informs her that he'll deliver the sad news. But before he can share it, Nora stalls him, thinking that sharing such news in the early morning is quite shameful. Still, she invites him to sit with her, but Peters tries to decline, as he wants to get this over. Despite his refusal, Nora's persistence gets to him, so he eventually complies. Upon sitting, he quickly reveals that her husband has passed away. Instead of mourning, Nora opines that Herb's passing is unsurprising, especially since he's not cautious. While she explains, Nora becomes touchy towards Peters, making him uncomfortable. Because of this, Peters tries to leave immediately, but Nora stops him, telling him to accompany her for a while. Even with her seduction, Peters remains faithful in doing his job, so he quickly gets up and leaves. Meanwhile in Brandt, Stan angrily storms inside the conference room when he sees Neil with Aldo and Julie. He confronts Neil for having his troop barricade Peckham's roads. In Neil's defense, it's beyond his control, so he can do nothing about it. Despite his reason, Stan is keen on making his troop withdraw, but Julie interrupts him by informing him of Herb's fate. This surprises him, but since the new casualty is another colleague and friend of his, Stan furiously questions Neil why they should stay in town, although calms him down and requests him to listen to what Neil has to say. Once calm, Neil begins to ask him if he can cross a man with a horse. Confused, Stan evades his question as he finds it ridiculous. Since Stan keeps on antagonizing him, Neil shackles Ryze and expresses that he's serious. After realizing that he's let his temper get the best of him, Neil gets straight to the point by inquiring Stan about controlled mutations. However, Stan denies its possibility as he explains that they're not yet scientifically capable of altering genes and chromosomes. Neil acknowledges his statement, but he asks him if they can accelerate the process artificially. Again, Stan believes it's impossible, but Aldo thinks otherwise. Aldo explains his theory of artificially substituting hormones, but Stan negates it. Still, he mentions that maybe Dr. Norstad and his bees might've made it possible. After hearing this, Neil becomes alert and requests the two scientists to help him prove his theory. Moments later, Neil presents his theory, but Stan remains skeptical about it and even deems it a fiction. Neil tries to reason with him, but Stan is too stubborn to listen. When Stan leaves, Aldo comments that Neil's theory has few credible points, returning the man's hope. Because of this, Neil asks if he'll help him, and Aldo confirms that he will. Now finding his determination, Neil orders Aldo to turn his theory into analysis so that he can send it to his agency. Aldo promises to finish it as soon as possible. Still, before letting Neil leave, he opines that if his theory about mutation did occur, it should be detected by a GammaCat synthesizer. Neil chuckles as he has difficulty deciphering what he just said, so Julie explains it. From what she knows, a gamma-count synthesizer works like a camera, and the mutated cells have gamma radiation which registers on a special photosensitive plate. Hence, she strongly suggests they try it to prove Neil's theory. While Neil is still in a meeting with the scientists, they are unaware of another b-girl attack. This time, it happens near the border when a woman asks a soldier for help. Immediately, the soldier follows her, and the woman tells him she has lost her keys. She points toward the possible location of the keys, and the soldier heads to it. Unbeknownst to him, he has fallen prey to a b-girl. During Grubowski's funeral, Neil and Julie notice the deceased men's wives are wearing similar outfits, especially their sunglasses. Julie takes the initiative of using the gamma count synthesizer
and successfully detects gamma radiation from them. Inside Stan's residence, his wife Harriet
tries to seduce him. Since their marriage lacks intimacy, Stan immediately falls for her charms.

While in the act, Stan realizes that Harriet is a b-girl upon seeing her black compound eyes. Before she can end his life, Stan grabs her tights and suffocates her with it. After the funeral, Julie studies the information she gathered earlier when Susan visits her. Julie tries to act nonchalant, but Susan reveals she's aware of what she's discovered, so she wants to explain it to her. Regardless, Julie refuses to listen as she believes her explanation won't suffice. Because of Julie's stubbornness, Susan entices her to visit her lab, promising her she'll be satisfied with what she'll show her. Convinced, Julie follows Susan to her laboratory. On the other hand, Neil's currently at Aldo's residence listening to his voice recorder about the insect's mating habits. Suddenly, Neil hears Susan's voice in the recording, making him realize that the culprit for Aldo's passing was her. Because of this, he also realizes that Julie's life is at risk, and Susan is on a rampage to eliminate those who hinder her plans. As a result, Neil rushes to Brand and arrives just in time to stop Susan from turning Julie into a b-girl. However, Susan threatens to pull the lever that will end Julie's life if he doesn't leave the laboratory. Neil acts like he is leaving, but he immediately pulls out his gun and shoots the machinery, causing chaos inside the room. Neil grabs the unconscious Julie, but before he leaves, he traps the women inside the radiation chamber, ending their lives. Inside the sheriff's office, Peters interrogates Julie and is confused about the motive behind the b-girl's killing spree. Julie explains that Susan overlooked the fact that the radiation caused them to be infertile. But since they share DNA with bees who mate to reproduce, they've become crazy about mating as they can't produce offspring. After Julie's explanation, Neil is in a rush to take her home, so Peters dismisses them immediately. Before he can leave, Peters shakes his hands and expresses his hope to see him again, not as an agent but as a tourist. Shortly after, the couple share a meal inside Julie's residence, but Neil itches to bed her. However, Julie continuously talks about the events earlier, so he quickly gets up and lets her follow him. After arriving at the bedroom, Julie's still talking non-stop, leaving Neil to throw her on the bed and kiss her.
